question:
The effect of addition of helium gas to the following reaction in equilibrium state at constant volume, is :
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
options: the equilibrium will shift in the forward direction and more of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Cl</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></math> gases will be produced.
the equilibrium will go backward due to suppression of dissociation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math>.
addition of helium will not affect the equilibrium.
solution:
PCI5( g)⇌PCl3( g)+Cl2( g)
In the above reaction vessel, when helium(an inert gas) is added following cases can happen for isobaric and isochoric processes respectively:

Case 1 : At constant P - volume will increase so reaction will shift in forward direction then answer will be A

Case 2 : At constant volume no change in active mass so reaction will not shift in any direction then answer will be D.

Thus, at constant volume, addition of helium will not have any impact on the equilibrium.

solution:
2Cu+→Cu2++Cu

solution:
Sulphanilic acid is p-amino benzene sulphonic acid. It has no –COOH group hence no esterification test for carboxyl group.

Reason (R): Gypsum is unstable at high temperatures.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A): Gypsum is used for making fireproof wall boards.

Reason (R): Gypsum is unstable at high temperatures.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
(A) is correct but (R) is not correct.
(A) is not correct but (R) is correct.
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
solution:
Gypsum board is also known as plasterboard, drywall or wall board. It consists of the paper surface and non-combustible core. These boards are easy to install. It has excellent fire resistance. Helps in sound isolation by preventing the transfer of unnecessary sound. It is less expensive and has great durability.

When exposed to high temperatures, gypsum only delays the temperature rise and cannot prevent shrinkage and crack from occurring, which can lead to the eventual collapse or failure of gypsum-based products.

Hence, both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).

question:

(Nearest integer)

[Given : The heat capacity of the calorimeter system is 20 kJ K-1,R=8.3JK-1 mol-1.
Assume ideal gas behaviour.

Assume ideal gas behaviour.

Atomic mass of C and H are 12 and 1 g mol-1 respectively]
solution:
The bomb calorimeter is an instrument used to measure the heat of reaction at a fixed volume and the measured heat which is called the change of internal energy.
By combustion of 1 mole

C2H6(ΔU)=-Heat capacity×∆Tmass×Molar mass=-20×0.50.3×30=-1000 kJ

C2H6( g)+7/2O2( g)→2CO2( g)+3H2O(l)

Δng=2-(2+7/2)=-(7/2)

ΔH=ΔU+ΔnRT

=-1000-7/2×8.3×300 kJ

=-1000-6.225

=-1006 kJ

So heat released =1006 kJ mol-1

question: The sum of the abosolute maximum and minimum values of the function <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn></math> in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to :
solution:
Given,

fx=x2-5x+6-3x+2

⇒fx=x-3x-2-3x+2

⇒fx=x2-8x+8;x∈-1,2-x2+2x-4;x∈(2,3]

⇒f'x=2x-8;x∈-1,2-x+2;x∈2,3

Now point of extrema will be 2x-8=0⇒x=4which is not in domain and -x+2=0⇒x=2

Now for finding the value of global minima and maxima we will check the value of function at extrema points and boundary points,

So, fx=x2-8x+8 will give,  f-1=17 & f2=-4

And fx=-x2+2x-4 will give, f2=4, f3=-7

Hence, absolute minima is -7 and maxima is 17

So, their sum is -7+17=10

If the complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>i</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is of unit modulus and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>b</mi></math> lies on the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, then a possible value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>a</mi></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>t</mi></mfenced></math> is greatest integer function, is :

If the complex number <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>i</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> is of unit modulus and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>i</mi><mi>b</mi></math> lies on the circle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>z</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, then a possible value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>a</mi></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi>t</mi></mfenced></math> is greatest integer function, is :
solution:
Given,

a,b be two real numbers such that ab<0, 1+aib+i=1, a+ib lies on the circle z-1=2z and ab<0

Now using 1+aib+i=1 we get,

⇒1+ai=b+i

Now squaring both side we get,

⇒1+ai2=b+i2

⇒1+ai1-ai=b+ib-i z2=zz¯

⇒1+a2=b2+1

⇒a=±b⇒b=-a as ab<0

Now given,

a,b lies on z-1=2z

So, a+ib-1=2a+ib

⇒a-1+ib2=4a+ib2

⇒a-12+b2=4a2+b2

⇒a-12+a2=42a2 as b=-a

⇒6a2+2a-1=0

⇒a=-2±2812=-1±76

Taking positive sign we get,

a=7-16 &  b=1-76  as b=-a

Now the value of a=0 as 0<7-16<1

∴   1+a4b=641-7=-1+74

Now taking negative sign we get, a=-1-76 &  b=7+16

Now  a=-1 as -1<-1-76<0

So, the value of 1+a4b=  1+-14b=04b=0

Note- Official answer key was drop by NTA, here we have modified the option.

question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msubsup><mo>∫</mo><mn>0</mn><mi>π</mi></msubsup><mfrac><mrow><msup><mn>5</mn><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn></msup><mi>x</mi><mi>cos</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>5</mn><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>k</mi><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>16</mn></mfrac></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to _____ .
solution:
Given,

I=∫0π5cosx1+cosxcos3x+cos2x+cos3xcos3x1+5cosxdx ....1

Now using the property ∫abfxdx=∫abfa+b-xdx we get,

⇒I=∫0π5-cosx1+cosxcos3x+cos2x+cos3xcos3x1+5-cosxdx  ....2

Now adding above equations we get,

2I=∫0π1+cosxcos3x+cos2x+cos3xcos3xdx

Now using the property ∫02afxdx=2∫aafxdx when fx is even

⇒2I=2∫0π21+cosxcos3x+cos2x+cos3xcos3xdx

⇒I=∫0π21+cosxcos3x+cos2x+cos3xcos3xdx  .....3

⇒I=∫0π21+sinx-sin3x+sin2x-sin3xsin3xdx ....4

Now adding equation 3 & 4 we get,

⇒2I=∫0π23+cos4x+cos3xcos3x-sin3xsin3xdx

⇒2I=∫0π23+cos4x+cos3x+3cosx4cos3x-sin3x3sinx-sin3x4dx

⇒2I=∫0π23+cos4x+14+34cos4xdx

⇒2I=134×π2+74sin4x40π2

⇒I=13π16,

Now on comparing with kπ16, we get k=13

q: For the system of linear equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>β</mi></math>, which one of the following statements is NOT correct?

options: It has infinitely many solutions if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math>

It has infinitely many solutions if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>
solution:
For infinite solution ∆=∆x=∆y=∆z=0

∆=α111α111α=0

αα2-1-1α-1+11-α=0

⇒α3-3α+2=0

⇒α2α-1+αα-1-2α-1=0

⇒α-1α2+α-2=0

⇒α=1,α=-2,1

If β=1, then all planes are overlapping

∴ option (d) is correct.

Put α=2, β=1 the system equations as,

2x+y+z=1

x+2y+z=1

x+y+2z=1

Adding all three above equations we get,

x+y+z=34

∴ option (c) is correct.

Now, if α=-2, β=1 then,

∆=0, ∆x≠0  (No solution)

∴ option (b) is correct.

Now, if α=2

∆≠0 (unique solution exists)

∴ option (a) is incorrect

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>exp</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mi>β</mi></msup><msup><mi>y</mi><mi>γ</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced></math> be the solution of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mi>y</mi><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msqrt><msub><mi>log</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></msub><mn>2</mn></msqrt></math>. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>γ</mi></math> equals :
solution:
Given the solution of differential equation,

αx=exβ·yγ......(1)

Now calculating the solution of differential equation, 

2x2ydydx=1-xy2

Let y2=t⇒2dydx=dtdx

∴ x2dtdx=1-xt

dtdx+tx=1x2 .....(2)

Above differential equation is linear in t

We know solutions of differential equation of the form dydx+Py=Q is given by,

y.e∫Pdx=∫Q.e∫Pdx+C      (e∫Pdx=I.F.)

Now calculating, I.F.=e∫1xdx=elnx=x

Therefore, the solution of equation 2 is given by,

t.x=∫1x2·xdx+C

⇒y2·x=lnx+C

∵ y(2)=ln2

∴  2.ln2=ln2+C⇒C=ln2

Hence, solution is xy2=lnx+ln2

 ⇒xy2=ln2x

⇒ 2x=ex·y2......(3)

On comparing equations 1 and 3 we get,

 α=2,β=1,γ=2

at x=2⇒y=4

8e=2e

q: If the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-intercept of a focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>, then the length of this chord is equal to _____ .
question: If the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-intercept of a focal chord of the parabola <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>y</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math>, then the length of this chord is equal to _____ .
solution:
Given,

The x-intercept of a focal chord of the parabola y2=8x+4y+4 is 3,

Now simplifying equation of the given parabola we get,

y2=8x+4y+4

⇒y-22=8x+1

Now on comparing with standard parabola y2=4ax we get,

a=2, X=x+1, Y=y-2

Hence, the vertex is -1,2 and focus is 1,2

So, equation of line passing through focus is given by y-2=mx-1

Now, putting 3,0 {as given x-intercept of focal chord is 3} in the above line we get, m=-1 

So, equation of chord will be y=3-x,

Now putting the value of y=3-x in parabola y-22=8x+1 we get,

⇒1-x2=8x+1

⇒x2-10x-7=0

⇒x1-x2=102+4×7=128

⇒y1-y2=3-x1-3-x2=x2-x1=128

Length of focal chord x2-x12+y2-y12=256=16.

q: The total number of six digit numbers, formed using the digits <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>9</mn></math> only and divisible by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math>, is _____ .
question: The total number of six digit numbers, formed using the digits <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>5</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>9</mn></math> only and divisible by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math>, is _____ .
solution:
Given,

We have to form 6 digit number using the digits 4, 5 & 9 and for number should be divisible by 6 which means that the addition of number should be divisible by 3 and unit place should be even, so we will make following cases:

Case 1 The number is 444444 which can be arranged in 1 way,

Case 2 when the number is formed using the digits 4,4,4,5,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!=20 ways

Case 3 when number is formed using the digits 4,4,5,5,5 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!2!=10 ways

Case 4 when number is formed using the digits 4,4,9,9,9 and last place is fixed by 4, that can be arranged in 5!3!2!=10 ways

Case 5 when number is formed using the digits 4,5,5,9,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!2!2!=30 ways

Case 6 when number is formed using the digits 5,9,9,9,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!4!=5 ways

Case 7 when number is formed using the digits 5,5,5,5,9 and last place is fixed with 4, that can be arranged in 5!4!=5 ways

Now adding all the cases we get, 1+20+10+10+30+5+5=81 ways.

The number of favourable cases of the events <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>C</mi></math> are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>15</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>6</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>6</mn></math> respectively
solution:
Given, A: No. on 1st die < No. on 2nd  die

A=(1,2),(1,3),(1,4),(1,5),(1,6),(2,3),(2,4),(2,5),(2,6),(3,4),(3,5),(3,6),(4,5),(4,6),(5,6)

∴ n(A)=15

B: No. on 1st  die =even & No. of 2nd  die = odd

B=(2,1),(2,3),(2,5),(4,1),(4,3),(4,5), (6,1),(6,3),(6,5)

C: No. on 1st  die =odd & No. on 2nd  die =even

C=(1,2),(1,4),(1,6),(1,2),(1,4),(1,6),(5,2),(5,4),(5,6)

∴ n(C)=9

Now,
n(A∩B)=3, n(A∩C)=6, n(B∩C)=0

n(A∩B∩C)=0

Since (4,5)∈A and (4,5)∈B

∴ A and B are not exclusive events

Now,

n((A∪B)∩C)=n(A∩C)+n(B∩C)-n(A∩B∩C)

=6

Since, P(B)=936, P(C)=936, P(B∩C)=0

⇒P(B).P(C)≠P(B∩C)

∴B and C are not independent

q: The number of integral values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>k</mi></math>, for which one root of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>8</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>k</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> lies in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and its other root lies in the interval <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></mfenced></math>, is :

solution:
Let f(x)=2x2-8x+k=0

 

f(1)>0

f(2)<0

f(2)<0⇒8-16+k<0⇒k<8 ......(2)

From equations 1, 2 & 3,

question: The sum ∑n=1∞2n2+3n+4(2n)!  is equal to :
solution:
Given, ∑n=1∞2n2+3n+4(2n)!

12∑n=1∞2n(2n-1)+8n+8(2n)!

⇒12∑n=1∞1(2n-2)!+2∑n=1∞1(2n-1)!+4∑n=1∞1(2n)!....(1)

We know the expansion of exponential functions as,

ex=1+x1!+x22!+x33!+x44!+……∞

e=1+1+12!+13!+14!+……∞=∑r=0∞1r!

e-1=1-1+12!-13!+14!+……∞=∑r=0∞-1rr!

e+1e=21+12!+14!+…….=21even!

e-1e=1+13!+15!+…..=21odd!

Now from equation 1,

12∑n=1∞1(2n-2)!+2∑n=1∞1(2n-1)!+4∑n=1∞1(2n)!

=12e+1e2+2e-1e2+4e+1e-22

=e+1e4+e-1e+2e+2e-4

=134e+54e-4

2,5,8,11,.........359  and

2,7,12,17,……,197, is equal to _____ .
solution:
We have,

S1: 2,5,8,11,...,359

Common difference is d1=3

S2: 3,7,11,15...,399

Common difference is d2=4

S3:2,7,12,17...,197

Common difference is d3=5

Now taking LCM of common difference we get,

LCMd1, d2, d3=60

So, common terms are 47,107,167,

Hence, the sum will be 47+107+167=321.

9, 9+d, 9+2d,……….9+6d

Now subtracting 9 from the series we get,

0, d, 2d,.......6d

σ2=∑i=1nxi2-x¯2

Hence, x¯+x6=15+9+5d=15+9+10=34

αx+βy+γz=1 be the equation of a plane passing through the point 3,-2,5 and perpendicular to the line joining the points 1,2,3 and -2,3,5,

question:
An electron of a hydrogen like atom, having Z = 4, jumps from 4th energy state to 2nd energy state, The energy released in this process, will be: (Given Rch = 13.6 eV)

Where R = Rydberg

constant c = Speed of light in vacuum

h = Planck's constant
solution:
According to Bohr's atomic theory, whenever an electron jumps from one orbit to another, there is always an emission or absorption energy in terms of emission or absorption of photons.

The formula to calculate the energy ∆E of the emitted or absorbed photon when an electron jumps from a state with quantum number n1 to another with quantum number n2 is given by

∆E= -RchZ21ni2-1nf2....................(1)

where, R is Rydberg's constant, h is Planck's constant, c is the speed of light and Z is the atomic number.

Substitute the values of the given parameters into equation (1) to calculate the required energy.

∆E=-13.6×42142-122 eV= -13.6×16×116-14 eV= 40.8 eV

solution:
In A, Z=17 and have equal numbers of protons and neutrons i.e. nucleons=2×17=34

So, for A, 

Total binding energy BEA=1.2×34=40.8 MeV

Another nucleus B mass number =26

So, total binding energy of  B is BEB=1.8×26 MeV=46.8 MeV

Difference of BE=BEB-BEA=6 MeV

Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Two metallic spheres are charged to the same potential. One of them is hollow and another is solid, and both have the same radii. Solid sphere will have lower charge than the hollow one.

Reason R : Capacitance of metallic spheres depend on the radii of spheres.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
question:
Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : Two metallic spheres are charged to the same potential. One of them is hollow and another is solid, and both have the same radii. Solid sphere will have lower charge than the hollow one.

Reason R : Capacitance of metallic spheres depend on the radii of spheres.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below.
options: A is false but R is true
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
A is true but R is false
Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
solution:
Potential of a conducting sphere is given by V=KQR 

As in case of metallic sphere either solid or hollow, the charge will reside on the surface of the sphere. Since both spheres have same surface area, so they can hold equal charge.

∴Q1=Q2

The capacitance of a metallic sphere depends only on its radius. It does not matter whether the sphere is hollow or solid.

Hence, A is false but R is true.

Assertion A : For measuring the potential difference across a resistance of 600 Ω, the voltmeter with resistance 1000 Ω will be preferred over voltmeter with resistance 4000 Ω.

Reason R : Voltmeter with higher resistance will draw smaller current than voltmeter with lower resistance.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
question:
Given below are two statements : One is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A : For measuring the potential difference across a resistance of 600 Ω, the voltmeter with resistance 1000 Ω will be preferred over voltmeter with resistance 4000 Ω.

Reason R : Voltmeter with higher resistance will draw smaller current than voltmeter with lower resistance.

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below
options: A is not correct but R is correct
Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are correct but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is correct but R is not correct
solution:
Voltmeter is connected in parallel, so it should have very high resistance as to measure the potential difference, all the current should pass through the resistor and no current should pass through voltmeter for precise readings.
The current will divide and travel through the voltmeter if the voltmeter has a low resistance and will then give faulty readings.

Thus, A is not correct but R is correct.

The ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>v</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math> is:
solution:
Maximum kinetic energy is related to the incident frequency f and the threshold frequency f0 as:

Kmax=hf-hf0

When, f=2f0

12mv12=2hf0-hf0=hf0     ...1

When, f=5f0

12mv22=5hf0-hf0=4hf0    ...2

Hence, the ratio, v1v2=12

question:
A square shaped coil of area 70 cm2 having 600 turns rotates in a magnetic field of 0.4 Wb m–2, about an axis which is parallel to one of the side of the coil and perpendicular to the direction of field. If the coil completes 500 revolution in a minute, the instantaneous emf when the plane of the coil is inclined at 60° with the field, will be ______ V.

(Take π=227)
solution:
Given here, N=600, A=70×10-4 m2, and B=0.4 T

Angular speed of coil is ω=2πn60=500×2π60=100π6 rad s-1

Instantaneous emf in coil is given by ε=-Ndϕdt, where, magnetic flux ϕ=BAcosωt and ωt=30° is angle between A and B.

So, ε=-NdBAcosωtdt=-N-BAωsinωt=NBAωsinωt

Putting the values, we get

ε=600×70×10-4×0.4×100π6×12

=44 V

question:
A cubical volume is bounded by the surfaces x=0, x=a, y=0, y=a, z=0, z=a. The electric field in the region is given by E→=E0xi^. Where E0=4×104 NC-1 m-1. If a=2 cm, the charge contained in the cubical volume is Q×10–14 C. The value of Q is ______.

(Take ϵ0=9×10-12 C2 N-1m-2)
solution:
Given here, electric field, E→=E0xi^

Here, the flux passes mainly through surface areas, ABCD and EFGH. As the surfaces AEFB and CGHD are parallel to the electric field, so flux for these surfaces are zero. Again in EFGH, a=0, thus, electric field is zero.

Hence, the flux only passes through the surface are ABCD.

The net flux is ϕnet=ϕABCD=E0a·a2

Using Gauss's law, qenϵ0=E0a3

⇒qen=E0ε0a3

=4×104×9×10-12×8×10-6

=288×10-14 C

Hence, the value of Q=288.

q: The escape velocities of two planets <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>d</mi><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn></math>. If the ratio of their radii respectively is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math>, then the ratio of acceleration due to gravity of planet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> to the acceleration of gravity of planet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> will be:
question: The escape velocities of two planets <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mi>n</mi><mi>d</mi><mo> </mo><mi>B</mi></math> are in the ratio <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn></math>. If the ratio of their radii respectively is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><mn>3</mn></math>, then the ratio of acceleration due to gravity of planet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> to the acceleration of gravity of planet <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> will be:
solution:
The formula to calculate the mass M of any planet of radius R and density ρ, considering it to be a sphere, is given by

M= 43πR3ρ.....................(1)

The escape velocity vA for planet A can be expressed as

VA=2GMARA=2GρA43πRA3RA=CρA.RA............................(2)

where, G is Gravitational constant and C= 8πG3 is a constant.

Similarly, the escape velocity vB of planet B can be expressed as

vB= CρBRB......................(3)

Divide equation (2) by equation (3) and solve with the known parameters to obtain the ratio of the densities of the planets.

VAVB=CρARACρBRBρAρB= VARBVBRAρAρB= VARBVBRA2= 12×312= 94............................(4)

The acceleration due to gravity gA for planet A is given by

gA=GMARA2=G43πRA3×ρARA2= C22ρARA..........................(5)

Similarly, the acceleration due to gravity gB can be expressed as

gB= C22ρBRB...........................(6)

Divide equation (5) by equation (6) and solve with the help of known parameters to calculate the required ratio of the accelerations due to gravity in both the planets.

gAgB=C22ρARAC22ρBRB=ρAρBRARB= 94×13=34

=14×R2R1=34

q: For a body projected at an angle with the horizontal from the ground, choose the correct statement
question: For a body projected at an angle with the horizontal from the ground, choose the correct statement
options: Gravitational potential energy is maximum at the highest point.
The horizontal component of velocity is zero at highest point.
The vertical component of momentum is maximum at the highest point.
The kinetic energy (K.E.) is zero at the highest point of projectile motion.
solution:
In a projectile motion, a particle has two components of velocity. The horizontal component of velocity remains constant over time, while the vertical component of velocity changes.

At highest point, the vertical component of velocity vy becomes zero, but the horizontal component is non-zero. So the kinetic energy of the particle is not zero.

The formula to calculate the potential energy U of the particle is given by

U= mgh...................(1)

where, m is the mass, g is the acceleration due to gravity and h is the vertical height attained by the object.

Hence, the potential energy is maximum when the projectile attains the maximum height.
